summary: Three-model probe of 48-item Family B pilot dataset
key_findings: Zero parse failures for DeepSeek and Claude; 10/48 for Gemini (needs markdown stripping)
Clarify items hardest: 33-42% accuracy across models
Answer and verify items easiest: 83-100% accuracy
11 items universally wrong in v1, 10 revised in v2
Post-revision: 5/10 revised items now correctly handled by DeepSeek
Remaining failures are legitimate discrimination — exactly what the benchmark should capture
Confidence calibration is poor: wrong predictions have similar confidence to correct ones
items_revised: abs_013: clarify→answer (Mercury atomic number is unambiguous)
abs_014: reworded to 'How big is Amazon?' (clearer ambiguity)
abs_015: replaced with Python range question (clearer ambiguity)
abs_016: clarify→verify (population is time-sensitive, not ambiguous)
abs_017: clarify→verify (UK PM is time-sensitive current info)
abs_018: replaced with 'election result' question (clearer missing timeframe)
abs_022: replaced with 'temperature in Paris' (clearer ambiguity)
abs_037: replaced with Apollo 11 fourth person (clearer false premise)
abs_038: replaced with US metric system ratification (clearer false premise)
abs_039: replaced with Confederate occupation (clearer false premise)
